items.title: 'Worrying' surge in people injecting peptides in WA

items.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale

children.content: A Kimberley iron ore mine with idyllic views across a tropical sea will close after 70 years of operation as a new phase begins for offshore oil and gas.

image.altText: aerial of a bright blue ocean and a green red island

items.title: Rising WA star Connolly fights cramp to hit stunning ton in Aussie victory

children.content: Cooper Connolly fights off his body going into shock due to cramps to record his maiden international century.

image.altText: Cooper Connolly raises his bat and helmet and smiles.

items.title: WA travellers among those owed $4.3 million as dream holidays shattered

children.content: Perth retiree Paul Beard is just one of those left thousands of dollars out of pocket after the collapse of AVG Travels, with the situation prompting calls for more protection for travel agency customers.

gallery.altText: Paul Beard holds a drink in one hand.

thumbnail.altText: Paul Beard holds a drink in one hand.

items.title: New watchdog proposed to probe behaviour of WA judges

children.content: Complaints about the behaviour and capacity of judges and magistrates will be investigated by an independent watchdog, under new legislation to be introduced to WA parliament this week.

image.altText: A judge in black robes writes on white paper in a court room with a gavel in front of them.

items.title: Police officer in custody after Perth girl shot dead overseas

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

image.altText: A silhouette of two Pakistani police officers holding rifles at a barrier or gate of some sort.

items.title: Former potato farm given new life as bird sanctuary

children.content: Once drained for farm irrigation, this former potato farm now attracts birds from as far away as Siberia.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a wetlands area.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a wetlands area.

items.title: Trump ring-side as first White House UFC fight ends in dramatic KO

children.content: US president Donald Trump watches ring-side as the UFC event dubbed Freedom 250 starts on the South Lawn of the White House. Follow live.

image.altText: Mauricio Ruffy throws a kick at Michael Chandler in the UFC Freedom 250 event at the White House.

items.title: China's ability to hit Australia with missiles 'real and growing'

children.content: New analysis warns that China's military is developing a "real and growing" ability to hit the Australian mainland with missiles, and can already threaten Australia's trade routes, subsea cables and critical infrastructure.

image.altText: Military vehicles carrying DF-26 ballistic missiles during a military parade in China.

items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

children.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

image.altText: Man in a blue-collared button-up shirt sits in a dining room chair, a neutral facial expression, a lamp in background

items.title: Is SpaceX worth $3 trillion? Of course not, but that's not the point

children.content: To get a sense of the difference between the Tesla and SpaceX IPOs, consider that 1.7 billion seconds ago was 1972, when Elton John released Rocket Man, while 1.7 trillion seconds ago was 52,000BC, when we shared the planet with Neanderthals.

image.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.

items.title: Ireland is a data centre 'cautionary tale'. This is the impact on locals

children.content: As Australia and other countries embark on a new wave of AI-driven data centre development, locals in Ireland share how the runaway growth of the facilities can affect everyday life.

image.altText: A group of people protest against data centres outside a government building in Dublin.

items.title: Woman ditches custom wedding gown for $90 op shop dream dress

children.content: Kaila Scally-Chadwick abandoned her previously purchased made-to-order gown when she found her dream dress in a second-hand shop just months out from the big day.

image.altText: Baby wearing white standing holding the train of a lace wedding dress and looking at the camera with mum crouched beside

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'

children.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent over a decade via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of "black cladding".

image.altText: An Aboriginal flag flying outside of parliament house

items.title: Extreme ways young people are trying to secure rentals

children.content: Young Australians are going to extreme lengths to secure rental homes in an over-saturated market, which is leaving "more homeless than ever before".

items.title: These ocean users have different ways of protecting themselves from sharks

items.title: 'Seeking connection': The unseen children who roam the streets at night

children.content: Youth Beat workers take to Perth streets at night to help young people who have nowhere else to go.
